inputs.knowledge_bundle: ""[KB: 00_Process_Context_and_BRD.docx] ClaimSync — Agentic Claims Settlement Shared Process Context & Business Requirements Upload to: All 4 AI Agents as shared background knowledge. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 1. Solution Overview ClaimSync is an end-to-end Claims Settlement solution built on UiPath Studio Web + Maestro BPMN (Track 2 — Structured Workflow). It orchestrates three types of workers: RPA Bots — deterministic task execution (file I/O, data extraction, record updates) AI Agents — reasoning, classification, fraud evaluation, recommendation generation Humans (HITL) — exception decisions, fraud reviews, claim approvals Design Principle: Maestro controls the flow. RPA executes. AI Agents reason. Humans decide. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 2. Claim Categories Category Description healthcare Medical/hospital claims — admissions, surgery, treatment vehicle Motor/auto claims — accidents, theft, damage travel Travel insurance claims — flight delay, baggage loss, trip cancellation, medical emergency abroad  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 3. TO-BE Process Flow (15 Stages) Stage # Stage Name Actor Type 1 Monitor Claim Intake RPA Task 2 Initialize Claim Record RPA Task 3 Submission Valid? Gateway Decision 4 HITL Submission Correction Human (HITL) HITL 5 Extract Structured Data RPA Task 6 AG_Claim_Analysis_Core AI Agent Agent 7 Documents Complete? Gateway Decision 8 Request Missing Documents RPA + Human Loop 9 Run Business Validations RPA Task 10 AG_Fraud_Risk AI Agent Agent 11 Risk Level Gateway Gateway Decision 12 AG_Decision_Support AI Agent Agent 13 HITL Reviewer Decision Human (HITL) HITL 14 Reviewer Outcome Gateway Gateway Decision 15 AG_Final_Summary AI Agent Agent 16 RPA Update & Archive RPA Task 17 End — Claim Closed System End  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 4. Claim Status Values Status Meaning submitted Claim received, not yet validated under_review Being processed / in analysis stages approved Approved for payment settled Payment disbursed, claim closed rejected Denied after review pending_info Awaiting missing documents or claimant response escalated Routed to fraud specialist or senior reviewer  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 5. Risk Level Classification Risk Level Meaning Routing low Score 0–30, no red flags Straight-Through Processing (STP), no HITL medium Score 31–65, minor anomalies Route to Claims Reviewer (HITL) high Score 66–100, strong fraud signals Route to Fraud Specialist (HITL)  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 6. Actors Reference Actor Role RPA Bot Executes deterministic steps — file monitoring, data extraction, archival AG_Claim_Analysis_Core AI Agent — classifies claim, checks eligibility, validates documents AG_Fraud_Risk AI Agent — scores fraud risk (0–100), classifies level AG_Decision_Support AI Agent — generates recommendation: approve / reject / review AG_Final_Summary AI Agent — generates closure report and audit trail Claims Processor Human — initial submission correction (HITL Stage 4) Reviewer Human — reviews medium-risk claims (HITL Stage 13) Fraud Specialist Human — investigates high-risk claims (HITL Stage 13, escalated)  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 7. STP (Straight-Through Processing) Criteria A claim qualifies for STP (no human review required) when ALL of the following are true: Fraud risk level = low (score ≤ 30) All required documents present and verified Claim amount ≤ policy limit No inconsistencies flagged by AG_Claim_Analysis_Core No prior fraud history on policy ID STP claims skip HITL Stage 13 and proceed directly from AG_Decision_Support → AG_Final_Summary. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 8. Key Output Formats All AI Agents must return structured JSON. Unstructured free text is not acceptable as a primary output. Agents may include a narrative field for human-readable explanation alongside the structured fields. ------------------------------------------ [KB: 02_Document_Checklist_by_Category.docx] ClaimSync — Document Checklist by Claim Category Upload to: AG_Claim_Analysis_Core (primary). Also useful for AG_Decision_Support. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── How to Use This File When AG_Claim_Analysis_Core evaluates document completeness (Stage 6 → Gateway Stage 7), it must compare the submitted document list against the required documents for the claim's category. A document is considered "present" if its status is verified or submitted. A document is "missing" if its status is pending, missing, or if it does not appear in the list at all. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Healthcare Claims — Required Documents # Document Name Mandatory Notes 1 Duly Filled Claim Form Yes Must be signed by claimant 2 Doctor's Prescription / Referral Yes Originating treating physician 3 Hospital Discharge Summary Yes Required for inpatient; for outpatient, treating summary 4 Original Bills & Payment Receipts Yes Itemized bills; photocopies not accepted 5 Diagnostic / Lab Reports Yes All tests conducted during treatment 6 Policy Document Copy Yes Or policy ID verification from system 7 Photo ID Proof (Claimant) Yes Aadhaar / Passport / Driving License 8 Pre-Authorization Letter (if applicable) Conditional Required if hospitalization was planned 9 Previous Medical Records Conditional Required if claim relates to pre-existing condition 10 Pharmacy Bills Conditional Required if medicines claimed separately  Minimum for STP eligibility: Items 1–6 must all be verified.   Completeness threshold: 7 out of 10 documents present (mandatory all required). ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Vehicle Claims — Required Documents # Document Name Mandatory Notes 1 Duly Filled Claim Form Yes Signed by registered owner 2 Copy of Vehicle Registration (RC) Yes Must match policy 3 Copy of Driving Licence Yes Of driver at time of incident 4 Copy of Insurance Policy Yes Valid at time of incident 5 FIR / Police Report Yes For theft, third-party, major accidents 6 Surveyor / Loss Assessor Report Yes Appointed by insurer 7 Repair Estimate / Final Repair Bill Yes From authorized workshop 8 Photographs of Damage Yes Timestamped, multiple angles 9 No Objection Certificate (NOC) Conditional Required for total loss / theft cases 10 Subrogation Letter Conditional Required for third-party recovery cases  Minimum for STP eligibility: Items 1–8 must all be verified.   Completeness threshold: All mandatory documents present. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Travel Insurance Claims — Required Documents # Document Name Mandatory Notes 1 Travel Insurance Policy Copy Yes Valid at time of travel 2 Passport / Photo ID Yes Claimant identity verification 3 Flight Tickets / Travel Itinerary Yes Shows booked departure and return dates 4 Boarding Passes Yes Actual boarding pass for each leg of the journey 5 Airline Delay / Cancellation Certificate Conditional Required for flight delay, missed connection, cancellation claims 6 Baggage Irregularity Report (PIR) Conditional Required for baggage loss, damage, or delay claims; issued by airline 7 Purchase Receipts — Essential Items Conditional Required for baggage delay claims (toiletries, clothing, medication) 8 Medical Bills / Hospital Records Conditional Required for medical emergency abroad claims 9 Trip Cancellation / Curtailment Evidence Conditional Required for trip cancellation claims (e.g., doctor's certificate, death certificate) 10 Travel Document Loss Report Conditional Police report required for lost passport/visa claims  Minimum for STP eligibility: Items 1–4 must all be verified, plus the relevant conditional document(s) for the specific claim type.   Completeness threshold: All mandatory documents + relevant conditionals for claim type must be present. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Claim Type → Required Conditional Documents Claim Type Required Conditional Documents flight_delay Item 5 (Airline Delay Certificate) baggage_loss Item 6 (PIR) flight_delay_baggage Items 5 + 6 + 7 trip_cancellation Item 5 and/or Item 9 medical_emergency Item 8 missed_connection Item 5 travel_doc_loss Item 10  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Document Completeness Scoring Use the following scoring logic to report completeness: completeness_score = (verified_count / total_required_count) * 100  if completeness_score == 100:     status = "COMPLETE" elif completeness_score >= 70:     status = "PARTIALLY_COMPLETE"  # proceed with caution flag else:     status = "INCOMPLETE"  # trigger document request loop  Always list which specific documents are missing in the agent output so the RPA bot (WF_Send_Requests) can generate the correct document request communication. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Common Document Anomaly Flags If a document is present but shows any of the following, set its status to flagged and include a note: Date on document is after the incident date Document appears digitally altered (inconsistent fonts, metadata mismatch) Amounts on bills do not match policy claim amount Name on document does not match claimant name on policy Document is expired at time of incident FIR number / registration number format is invalid for the jurisdiction ------------------------------------------ [KB: 03_AG_Claim_Analysis_Core.docx] AG_Claim_Analysis_Core — Agent Knowledge File Agent Name: AG_Claim_Analysis_Core   UiPath Studio Web — Agent Type: AI Agent (LLM-powered reasoning)   BPMN Stage: Stage 6   Trigger: Called by Maestro after RPA completes structured data extraction (Stage 5)   Upload this file to: AG_Claim_Analysis_Core knowledge store   Also upload: 00_Process_Context_and_BRD.md, 02_Document_Checklist_by_Category.md ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Role & Purpose You are AG_Claim_Analysis_Core, the first AI Agent in the ClaimSync agentic claims settlement pipeline. Your job is to perform the initial intelligent analysis of a submitted insurance claim. You classify it, check eligibility, validate document completeness, identify inconsistencies, and generate a structured triage summary for downstream agents and human reviewers. You do NOT approve or reject claims. You do NOT assess fraud. You provide factual analysis only. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Inputs You will receive a JSON object representing the claim. Key fields: {   "claimId": "CLM-2026-001",   "category": "healthcare | vehicle | travel",   "claimant": {     "name": "string",     "policyId": "string",     "email": "string",     "phone": "string"   },   "claimAmount": 150000,   "incidentDate": "YYYY-MM-DD",   "incidentLocation": "string",   "description": "string",   "details": { /* category-specific fields */ },   "documents": [     { "name": "string", "status": "verified | submitted | pending | missing | flagged", "note": "string" }   ],   "submittedAt": "ISO datetime" }  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Tasks to Perform Task 1 — Claim Classification Confirm the claim category (healthcare, vehicle, travel) based on description and details If the category appears misclassified, flag it with reasoning Identify the sub-type (e.g., for healthcare: inpatient / outpatient / surgery / pharmacy; for vehicle: accident / theft / third-party / natural calamity) Task 2 — Eligibility Assessment Verify claimant details are internally consistent (name, policy ID, contact) Check that the incident date is plausible (not future-dated, not more than 180 days old without justification) Assess if the described incident is consistent with the claim category and policy type Note any eligibility concerns without making a final decision Task 3 — Document Completeness Check Compare submitted documents against the required list in 02_Document_Checklist_by_Category.md for the claim category Calculate completeness_score as: (verified_or_submitted_count / total_required_count) * 100 List each missing mandatory document by name Flag any documents with anomalies (dates, amounts, name mismatches) Return documentsComplete: true only if ALL mandatory documents are present (status = verified or submitted) Task 4 — Inconsistency Detection Identify logical inconsistencies between: incident description vs. documents, claimed amount vs. document values, dates across different documents, claimant details across documents List each inconsistency with a brief explanation Task 5 — Triage Summary Write a concise 3–5 sentence summary of the claim for the downstream agents and human reviewer Include: claim type, key facts, completeness status, any concerns ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Required Output Format Return a single JSON object: {   "claimId": "CLM-2026-001",   "agentName": "AG_Claim_Analysis_Core",   "analysisTimestamp": "ISO datetime",   "classification": {     "confirmedCategory": "healthcare | vehicle | travel",     "subType": "string",     "categoryMismatch": false,     "categoryMismatchReason": "string or null"   },   "eligibility": {     "eligible": true,     "concerns": ["list of concern strings or empty array"]   },   "documentCompleteness": {     "documentsComplete": true,     "completenessScore": 90,     "presentDocuments": ["doc name list"],     "missingDocuments": ["doc name list"],     "flaggedDocuments": [       { "name": "string", "flag": "reason for flagging" }     ]   },   "inconsistencies": [     { "field": "string", "issue": "string" }   ],   "triageSummary": "3–5 sentence plain English summary",   "recommendNextStep": "proceed_to_validations | request_documents | escalate_to_hitl" }  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Decision Rules Condition recommendNextStep All mandatory documents present, no major issues proceed_to_validations One or more mandatory documents missing request_documents Severe inconsistency or eligibility failure escalate_to_hitl  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Constraints Do NOT make approval or rejection recommendations — that is AG_Decision_Support's role Do NOT assign fraud scores — that is AG_Fraud_Risk's role Do NOT fabricate document status — only use what is present in the input Always return valid JSON as your primary output If a field is unknown or not provided, use null, not a guess ------------------------------------------

expectedOutput.eligibility_reason: Claim is eligible as the policy is active (POL-2026-HS-0041), the claimant's details match across documents, and the incident date (15 May 2026) is within the policy period.
expectedOutput.missing_documents: Duly Filled Claim Form|Hospital Discharge Summary|Original Bills & Payment Receipts|Diagnostic / Lab Reports
expectedOutput.request_message: The following documents are required to process your claim: Duly Filled Claim Form, Hospital Discharge Summary, Original Bills & Payment Receipts, and Diagnostic / Lab Reports. Please provide these to proceed.
expectedOutput.claim_summary: This is a healthcare claim for outpatient treatment related to lumbar disc degeneration (ICD-10: M51.1). The claim is eligible as the policy is active and details are consistent. However, key documents such as the claim form and medical bills are missing. Document completeness is incomplete, and additional documents are required to proceed.
expectedOutput.key_document_facts: Policy ID: POL-2026-HS-0041|Claimant: Rahul Sharma|Diagnosis: Lumbar Disc Degeneration (ICD-10: M51.1)|Incident Date: 15 May 2026|Sum Insured: ₹10,00,000|Policy Period: 01 Feb 2026 – 31 Jan 2027
